title: HUD, Text, Menus, Focus, Multichannel Cues, and Motion Settings · Transfer Build
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers hud, text, menus, focus, multichannel cues, and motion settings into a changed accessible responsive game interface and settings flow. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of accessible responsive game interface and settings flow with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through…
objectives: Choose scalable fonts, line spacing, contrast, alignment, safe areas, localization space, and structured labels for essential text.
Support predictable keyboard and controller navigation, wrap policy, back, confirmation, cancellation, and no traps.
Render persistent non-color focus and announce or retain important state, errors, destructive actions, and setting changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Readable Text
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 1571 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to build readable keyboard and controller menus, HUD, focus, status, multi-channel cues, text scaling, and reduced-motion options; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose scalable fonts, line spacing, contrast, alignment, safe areas, localization space, and structured labels for essential text.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Motion Photosensitivity
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-motion-photosensitivity-bfvs2e" that uses case 7167 to build readable keyboard and controller menus, HUD, focus, status, multi-channel cues, text scaling, and reduced-motion options. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Motion Photosensitivity.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Motion Photosensitivity into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Motion Photosensitivity, keep Motion Photosensitivity connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-motion-photosensitivity-bfvs2e
# Competency: asteroids-motion-photosensitivity.
# Case 7167: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_motion_photosensitivity_bfvs2e(critical_signals: int = 8, multi_channel_signals: int = 8, focusable_controls: int = 7, focused_controls: int = 7, reduced_motion: bool = True, scalable_text: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_bfvs2e = "bfvs2e-e2svfb-7167"
    assert evidence_variant_bfvs2e
    evidence_axes_bfvs2e = (
        "playerbfvs2e",
        "seed7167",
        "inpute2svfb",
        "framebfvs2e7167",
        "state7167bfvs2e",
        "playteste2svfb7167",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_bfvs2e)) == 6
    if critical_signals < 1 or focusable_controls < 1:
        return False, critical_signals, "ui-domain"
    if multi_channel_signals != critical_signals:
        return False, multi_channel_signals, "color-only-signal"
    if focused_controls != focusable_controls:
        return False, focused_controls, "focus-gap"
    if not reduced_motion or not scalable_text:
        return False, focusable_controls, "display-access"
    assert focused_controls == focusable_controls
    return True, focused_controls, "accessible-game-ui"
